subject: Cloud Computing
title: Single Sign-On for Cloud
description: A short lesson to help you apply Single Sign-On for Cloud.

hook.text: Your company uses 100 SaaS tools. Without SSO, that's 100 separate passwords per employee.

buildup.text: Single Sign-On lets users log in once and access many applications. One identity, many services.

discovery.text: Providers like Okta and Azure AD federate identity across cloud apps using protocols like SAML and OIDC.

twist.text: SSO is a single point of failure. If the identity provider goes down, nobody can log into anything.

climax.text: SSO reduces password fatigue and improves security. But it demands that the identity provider is rock-solid.

punchline.text: One login for everything. Protect that one login with your life.

quiz.question: What is a risk of relying on Single Sign-On?
options: If the identity provider goes down, access to all connected services is lost
SSO requires users to remember more passwords than before
Only one user can be logged in at a time across the company
